direction.audience_fit: Architecture, security, observability, runtime systems, and dramatic technical demos.

typography.heading: Aptos, Avenir Next, Helvetica Neue, Arial, sans-serif
typography.body: Aptos, Avenir Next, Helvetica Neue, Arial, sans-serif
typography.mono: SF Mono, Cascadia Code, Consolas, monospace

patterns.title_slide: High-contrast technical title with restrained runtime glow.
patterns.section_slide: Dark transition slide with short labels and clear contrast.
patterns.comparison_slide: Two bordered panels with risk/accent top rules.
patterns.table_slide: Compact but readable table on dark surface.
patterns.code_slide: Near-black code panel with large enough mono text.
patterns.architecture_slide: Few nodes, high-contrast labels, clear trust or runtime boundaries.

avoid: using dark mode for conservative or print-first decks
accessibility: diagram labels must not rely on color alone

decisions.decision: Reserve dark runtime for technical decks where the setting benefits from it.
decisions.rationale: Dark mode can make system flows feel focused, but it should not be the default for all presentations.
